name: Cadabra
description: A computer algebra system specifically designed for problems in classical and quantum field theory, general relativity, and tensor algebra.
whyUseIt: Cadabra handles the complex tensor algebra and index manipulation that arises in general relativity and field theory — tasks that are extremely tedious and error-prone by hand. It understands symmetries, commutation relations, and the Einstein summation convention natively.
fieldPosition: Theoretical physics — tensor calculus for general relativity and field theory
physicistWorkflow: Define the metric and coordinates, compute connection coefficients and curvature tensors, simplify using symmetries, and export to LaTeX.
supervisorExpects: Correct and efficient tensor algebra computations for general relativity or field theory research.
computationalImpact: Eliminates pages of error-prone hand calculation in tensor algebra — essential for general relativity research.

vocabulary.definition: A mathematical object with indices that transforms in a specific way under coordinate changes
The connection coefficients that describe how the coordinate basis changes from point to point in curved spacetime

beginnerMistakes: Not declaring index properties (symmetries, ranges) correctly
Forgetting to specify the metric signature
